heading: Allotment formula
label: 927 Allotment formula
notes: References in TextThe Individuals with Disabilities Education Act, referred to in subsec. (c)(1), (2), is title VI of , , . Parts B and C of the Act are classified generally to subchapters II (§ 1411 et seq.) and III (§ 1431 et seq.), respectively, of chapter 33 of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see and Tables.Pub. L. 91–23084 Stat. 175section 1400 of this titleApr. 13, 1970

L. 102–119, § 27105 Stat. 608Oct. 7, 1991“(a)8, 9, 10.—SectionsandThe amendments made by sections 8, 9, and 10 [amending sections 1423, 1431, 1434, and 1435 of this title] shall take effect on , or on the date of enactment of this Act [], whichever is later.October 1, 1991Oct. 7, 1991“(b)5, 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, 18.—SectionsandThe amendments made by sections 5, 12, 13, 14, 15, 17, and 18 [amending sections 1413, 1472, 1476, 1477, 1478, 1480, and 1482 of this title] shall take effect , except that each State shall have the option to have any of the amendments apply earlier than such date.July 1, 1992“(c).—Remaining ProvisionsThe remaining sections of this Act [enacting , amending this section, sections 241, 1087ee, 1400 to 1402, 1404, 1405, 1407, 1411 to 1417, 1419, 1421 to 1425, 1431 to 1433, 1435, 1442, 1443, 1451, 1452, 1461, 1471 to 1473, and 1475 to 1485 of this title, sections 2503 and 2504 of Title 25, Indians, sections 721, [former] 774, 777a, 795m, and 796d of Title 29, Labor, and sections 1396b, 1396n, 6022, 6024, 9835, 9855d, 9862, and 9886 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare, and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section and sections 1400 and 1484 of this title] and the amendments made by such sections shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act [].”section 1484a of this titleOct. 7, 1991

children.heading: Establishment by regulation of minimum allotment formula; criteria
children.label: a Establishment by regulation of minimum allotment formula; criteria

children.text: the number of students served by a school and the size of the school;

children.label: B
children.text: a need for special staffing, transportation, or educational programs at the school, and
the cost of providing academic services of a high quality as required by ; andsection 921(b)(1) of this title
such other factors as the Director considers appropriate.

children.heading: Issuance, etc., of regulations
children.label: b Issuance, etc., of regulations
children.text: Any regulation under subsection (a) shall be issued, and shall become effective, in accordance with the procedures applicable to regulations required to be issued by the Secretary of Education in accordance with .section 1232 of this title

children.heading: Children with disabilities
children.label: 1 Children with disabilities
children.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of , the provisions of part B of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act [ et seq.], other than the funding and reporting provisions, shall apply to all schools operated by the Department of Defense under this chapter, including the requirement that children with disabilities, aged 3 to 5, inclusive, receive a free appropriate public education.section 921(b)(3) of this title20 U.S.C. 1411

children.heading: Infants and toddlers with disabilities
children.label: 2 Infants and toddlers with disabilities
children.text: The responsibility to provide comparable early intervention services to infants and toddlers with disabilities and their families in accordance with individualized family service plans described in section 636 of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act [] and to comply with the procedural safeguards set forth in part C of such Act [ et seq.] shall apply with respect to all eligible dependents overseas.20 U.S.C. 143620 U.S.C. 1431

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: 3 Implementation
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (2), the Secretary shall have in effect a comprehensive, coordinated, multidisciplinary program of early intervention services for infants and toddlers with disabilities among Department of Defense entities involved in the provision of such services to such individuals.
